titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gordon of Gordonstoun Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 4 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 4, 2025 at 23:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, legal documents, deeds, and other papers by or relating to the Gordon family of Gordonstoun and Cumming family of Altyre, Scotland.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Sotheby's via Winifred Meyers on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, circa 1955.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1614-1831. II. Other Papers, 1513-1948.

bioghist.head: Gordon family
bioghist.p: The Gordon family of Gordonstoun, Moray, Scotland, are part of Clan Gordon from Aberdeenshire. The chief of the clan is the Marquess of Huntly, earlier the Earl of Huntly. George Gordon (1514-1562), 4th Earl of Huntly, married Elizabeth Keith, and they had 12 children including George Gordon, 5th Earl of Huntly (-1576) and Lady Jean Gordon, Countess of Bothwell (1546-1629). Jean Gordon married Alexander Gordon, 12th Earl of Sutherland. Their children included Robert Gordon (1580–1656), a Scottish politician who acquired properties in the shires of Elgin and Forres that were united into the barony of Gordonstoun by charter in 1642. Gordon married Lucie (or Louise) Gordon (1597–1680), and their children included Lodovick (or Ludovick) Gordon (1614–1685), a member of parliament for Elgin & Forresshire, who married Elizabeth Farquhar, daughter of Sir Robert Farquhar of Mounie; and Katherine Gordon (1621–1683), who married Colonel David Barclay. Katherine and David Barclay's son Robert (1648-1690) was a writer belonging to the Religious Society of Friends and titular governor of the East Jersey colony in North America. The Gordonstoun baronetcy passed from Ludovick to his son Robert (1647–1704), grandson Robert (1696–1772), and great-grandson William, who died in 1795 and was succeeded by Alexander Penrose Cumming Gordon (1749-1806) of Altyre. Other members of the family included George Gordon (1643-1716), 4th Marquess of Huntly and 9th Earl of Huntly, who was created Duke of Gordon in 1684.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gordon of Gordonstoun Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
Reunited previously separated materials: Robert Farquhar, Thomas Urquhart, inidentified correspendence and unsorted documents (1660s, 1682) to the collection in 2024.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, legal documents, deeds, and other papers by or relating to the Gordon family of Gordonstoun and Cumming family of Altyre, Scotland. Correspondents include George Gordon, Robert Gordon, Alexander Penrose Cumming Gordon, Robert Farquhar of Mounie, Thomas Urquhart of Cromarty, King Charles I, King James I, and Oliver Cromwell. Topics of letters and papers are the Jacobite army and support for the rebellion in 1745-1746, smuggling off the coast of Scotland in the 1740s, the Napoleonic Wars, land investment in Scotland and Jamaica, and British colonization of India. The collection also contains passports issued by Charles I and King Louis XV of France.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gordon of Gordonstoun Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series largely consists of business and personal correspondence of the Gordon, Cumming, Farquhar, and Urquhart families. Among the topics of letters are smuggling on the Scottish coast, demands by the Jacobite army on Robert Gordon (1696-1772), administration of Alexander Cumming's estate in Jamaica, the Napoleonic Wars, and Great Britain's colonization of India in the 1800s. Correspondents include Robert Gordon, John Gordon, Robert Farquhar, Robert and Thomas Urquhart, King Charles I and King James I of England, and Oliver Cromwell, as well as Scottish slave trader and Jacobite supporter Evan Baillie.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Boyle, Roger, Earl of Orrery, 1621-1679, to the High Sheriff of Cromarty
Charles I, King of England, 1600-1649, to James Stuart, Duke of Lennox (1612-1655)
Charles I, King of England, 1600-1649, to Robert Gordon (1580-1656)
Cromwell, Oliver, 1599-1658, to the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland (copy)
Duc de Chartres [?] to Sir William and Lady Gordon Cummings
Gordon, George Gordon, Duke of, 1649-1716, to John Gordon and Lodovick Gordon
Gordon, Robert, 1580-1656, to Alexander Gordon and others
Gordon, Robert, Sir, 1696-1772. Memorial to Lord George Murray

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to demands on Gordon to provision the Jacobite army.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include British colonial rule of India.

did.unittitle: James I, King of England, 1566-1625, to Sir Robert Gordon (1580-1656)
Justices of the Peace for the County of Elgin & Forres
Lauderdale, John Maitland, Duke of, 1616-1682, to Alexander Lindsay, Earl of Balcarres, and Lord Provost of Edinburgh
Lawrie, Gawen (copy of 1684 letter regarding East Jersey province)
Lennox, James Stuart, Duke of, 1612-1655, to Robert Gordon
Lovat, Simon Fraser, Lord, 1667 or 1668-1747, to George Cumming
Macleod (copy of a letter from Fort Augustus)
Montrose, James Graham, Marquis of, 1612-1650, to Robert Gordon
Ogilvie, James, Earl of Seafield, 1750-1811, incoming letters in French and English
Pembroke, Philip Herbert, Earl of, 1584-1650, to Robert Gordon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: References Battle of La Guaira during the War of Jenkins' Ear.

did.unittitle: Urquhart, Thomas, 1611-1660, to Robert Farquhar and others
Unidentified author. "Answer to Ramsay his denyall ye 9 of december 1631"
Unidentified author. "A letter of an Independent, to his honored friend Mr Glyn recorder of London"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains passports issued by the British and French governments, documents related to smuggling off the Scottish coast, depositions concerning the Jacobite army, petitions, articles of agreement, commissions, papers concerning land and tenancy in Gordonstoun and Jamaica, and other legal and financial records dating from the 1500s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title, with unsorted and unidentified materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Account of arrears due by the tenants of the estate of Gordonstown

Act of admission of Sir Robert Gordon upon counsel
Act of synod [clergy] related to land

Battle of Philiphaugh, list of prisoners of war
Cumming, James. Certificate signed by Alexander Brodie Lyon
Declaration signed by Robert Gordon, Lodovick Gordon, Gilbert Keith, and others

Document referencing Rudolf II, Holy Roman Emperor, 1584 (copy)
Documents related to a cathedral church

England and Wales. Sovereign (1603-1625 : James I). Bound volume of historical and political tracts, papers found with volume
England and Wales. Sovereign (1625-1649 : Charles I). Passport for Robert Gordon
England and Wales. Sovereign (1660-1685 : Charles II). Passport for Lodovick Gordon

Gordon, Robert, 1580-1656. Documents related to or signed by
Gordon, Robert, 1580-1656. Petitions to Charles I, King of England
Gordon, Robert, 1580-1656, Lodovick Gordon, and Sir Robert Gordon (1696-1772). Documents related to or signed by
Gordon, Robert, 1647-1704. Articles of agreement with tradesmen
Henderson, Thomas. Fragment of document to an unidentified commission
Instructions and orders for officials … of the shires of Inverness, Sutherland, Caithness and Cromarty, Elgin …

Memorandum related to the estate and property of Alexander Cumming
Memorial concerning the mines of Scotland
Memorials and papers related to smuggling

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an extract of a court martial of George Cummings.

did.unittitle: Middleton, John Middleton, Earl of, 1619-1674. Document, signed
Murray, James. Documents related to or signed by
Murray, Thomas. Declaration related to Robert Gordon and John Gordon
